title: eritrichium howardii
intro: Eritrichium howardii, also known as Howard's beardtongue, is a flowering plant species native to the western United States. The plant is a member of the borage family and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Eritrichium howardii is said to offer various health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues, sore throats, and skin irritations, as well as acting as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, salves, or infusions, making it a versatile addition to traditional herbal remedies.
